behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: dispatcher must wire one shared document plus role-specific instruction packs into three reviewer invocations. This is not LLM-output quality, and no real OS/network/subprocess is required if reviewer clients are in-process fakes. Rejected higher levels: no behavior text requires real infrastructure or judging model content.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with fake reviewer adapters for extractor/risk/compliance, capture invocation payloads, assert all three receive byte-identical source document and each receives its own expected instruction pack.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment over contract text (extraction quality). Stubbed outputs would always pass and hide real misses/hallucinations. Rejected Workflow: only one live model decision is under test, not a multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Call the live clause extractor on contracts with known parties/dates/obligations/penalties and assert required terms are extracted with acceptable recall/precision thresholds.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is invocation wiring/serialization correctness across local modules, not risk-quality evaluation. In-process fakes can detect wrong/missing policy fields. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about parameter passing, not real model output or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke review pipeline with a distinctive risk policy fixture; intercept risk-assessor request via fake client and assert policy id, thresholds, and rule toggles are transmitted unchanged.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly tests semantic detection quality (including indirect/euphemistic language), which depends on real AI output. Mocking the model would hide this failure class. Rejected Workflow: single reviewer quality check, not chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Send curated contracts containing direct and euphemistic risky phrases to the live risk assessor under a fixed policy; assert expected risky passages are flagged and severity meets acceptance criteria.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module boundary (orchestrator/config -> compliance invocation payload). No need for real model judgment; fake compliance client can verify payload fidelity. Rejected higher levels because behavior text only requires correct passing of inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute pipeline with unique jurisdiction/profile fixture, intercept compliance-checker invocation, and assert all jurisdiction codes and regulatory profile fields are present and exact.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: This is model decision correctness against regulatory requirements; deterministic stubs cannot validate true pass/fail judgment quality. Rejected Workflow: single live checker decision is evaluated, not multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run live compliance checker on labeled contracts (compliant/non-compliant) across configured profiles and assert correct determinations plus expected requirement-level rationale coverage.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is merge logic correctness for conflict representation and attribution, which can be exercised as a pure function/object without I/O. Rejected Integration: no concrete cross-module dependency is required by this behavior text; conflict-preservation invariant lives inside merge logic itself.
behaviors.test_description: Call merge function with synthetic reviewer outputs containing same clause id and conflicting labels; assert unified report includes both assessments and correct reviewer attribution metadata.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans multiple agentic steps in sequence (three live reviewers + merge orchestration) and validates cross-step handoff/completeness of findings. Integration with stubs would miss failures caused by real model output shape/content interacting with orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline on real contract(s) with live extractor/risk/compliance reviewers, then verify merged report contains all findings emitted by each reviewer with no dropped entries and correct source attribution.
